01_initial_request.input_data: Labeled samples from seen classes, visual-semantic pairs, semantic prototypes, and popular ZSL/GZSL benchmark datasets
01_initial_request.user_intent: To identify unseen (novel) objects using only labeled samples from seen classes by improving visual-semantic alignment, representation quality, and alleviating prediction bias
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Significantly outperform state-of-the-art methods on popular benchmarks under ZSL and GZSL settings in terms of performance accuracy

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Zero-shot learning requires visual features and semantic prototypes. [Gap] Raw images need to be converted to high-dimensional features for processing. [Decision] To achieve robust visual representation, chose to use ResNet-101 to perform feature extraction on benchmark datasets, expecting to obtain 2,048-dimensional visual features.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Extracted 2,048-dimensional visual features for training and test sets.

references.relevance: Describes the use of ResNet-101 for visual feature extraction.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Contrastive representation learning improves downstream task performance. [Gap] Standard embeddings lack alignment and uniformity properties. [Decision] To achieve high quality representations, chose to use Contrastive Embedding with Alignment and Uniformity losses on hyperspherical space, expecting to obtain normalized features invariant to noise.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Visual features normalized and mapped to hyperspherical space with improved alignment and uniformity.

references.relevance: Defines the contrastive embedding with alignment and uniformity properties.
Inspiration for contrastive representation learning leading to better performance.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Discriminative information needs to be captured at the instance level. [Gap] Class-level supervision alone ignores instance-specific details. [Decision] To achieve better discrimination, chose to use Instance-level Contrastive Loss with K+1-way classification, expecting to obtain instance-wise constrained features.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Instance-level loss optimized, encouraging intra-class features to gather closely.

references.relevance: Formulates the instance-level loss with K negative samples and temperature tau.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Visual-semantic interaction is crucial for ZSL. [Gap] Shallow MLP structures limit representation quality. [Decision] To achieve desirable visual-semantic alignment, chose to use RNet (Relation Network) for class-level contrastive learning, expecting to obtain consistency scores between visual and semantic representations.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Class-level contrastive values computed between visual latent features and semantic features.

references.relevance: Describes RNet for conducting visual-semantic interactions.
Related method proposing a learnable metric for visual-semantic interaction.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Unseen classes lack visual supervision during training. [Gap] Models fail to learn optimal representations for unseen classes. [Decision] To achieve unbiased visual-semantic alignment for unseen classes, chose to use Regression Mechanism (Ridge Regression or Neural Networks), expecting to obtain transferable interactions from seen to unseen semantic prototypes.

parameters.methods: Neural Networks (512 dimension hidden layer) or Ridge Regression
parameters.loss_function: MSE for NN, regularization beta for Ridge

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Regression coefficients obtained to replace one-hot labels for unseen classes.

references.citation: Section 3.3 Regression mechanism for unseen classes
references.relevance: Introduces regression to compensate for lack of unseen supervision.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Prediction bias towards seen classes affects GZSL performance. [Gap] Standard classifiers do not distinguish domains effectively. [Decision] To alleviate prediction bias, chose to use Pluggable Calibrator (Softmax Classifier), expecting to obtain domain prior knowledge for test instances.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Calibrator trained to distinguish seen vs unseen domains via information entropy.

references.relevance: Describes the calibrator to adjust final decision scores.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Final predictions need calibration to reduce domain bias. [Gap] Raw contrastive values may be biased towards seen classes. [Decision] To achieve reduced prediction bias, chose to use Thresholding on Contrastive Values, expecting to obtain calibrated scores for both seen and unseen classes.

parameters.input: output rij of hierarchical contrastive learning
parameters.domain_distinction: threshold t on information entropy I

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Prediction scores calibrated, reducing misclassification between seen and unseen categories.

references.relevance: Explains how calibration is applied to contrastive values.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Model performance needs to be quantified on standard benchmarks. [Gap] Performance claims require empirical validation. [Decision] To achieve objective verification, chose to use Benchmark Evaluation on ZSL/GZSL settings, expecting to obtain accuracy metrics (T, S, U, H).

parameters.metrics: ZSL Accuracy (T), GZSL Accuracy (S, U), Harmonic Mean (H)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: HCR achieved SoTA results on SUN and FLO, and competitive results on AwA2 and APY.

references.relevance: Details the experimental setup and metrics.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Benchmark Accuracy Comparison on ZSL/GZSL Settings

ZSL_AwA2_Improvement.interpretation: HCR outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on AwA2 in terms of average per-class accuracy.

ZSL_FLO_Improvement.interpretation: HCR outperforms state-of-the-art approaches on FLO in terms of average per-class accuracy.

GZSL_SUN_H_Improvement.interpretation: HCR w/ RC improves Harmonic Mean on SUN compared to CC-ZSL.

GZSL_AwA2_U_Improvement.interpretation: Calibrator improves accuracy of unseen classes on AwA2.

GZSL_AwA2_S_Improvement.interpretation: Calibrator improves accuracy of seen classes on AwA2.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The proposed HCR significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods on popular benchmarks under ZSL and GZSL settings, achieving the quantifiable goal of improving visual-semantic alignment and alleviating prediction bias.
